title: Randia tetrasperma

It belongs to the genus Randia, which comprises several species of flowering plants. The plant's classification is further categorized as belonging to the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. intro: Randia tetrasperma, also known as the "Indian snake root," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antihyperglycemic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of diabetes, fever, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Randia tetrasperma include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, including randicine and randiavine, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Indian and Southeast Asian medicine preparations, such as Ayurvedic and Unani formulations.
